evals.prompt: I need an 8-week content calendar for our new B2B SaaS product launch -- we're releasing an AI-powered contract management platform called ClauseIQ targeted at mid-market legal ops teams (200-1000 employees). Campaign goal is demo requests. Channels: LinkedIn company page, our CEO's personal LinkedIn, blog, and email newsletter. We have 1 full-time content writer plus AI tooling for drafts. The product launches week 5 so we need pre-launch awareness weeks 1-4 and post-launch conversion content weeks 5-8. Our CEO is speaking at LegalTech Connect in week 3 -- that's a tentpole moment. Personas are (1) VP Legal Ops who cares about efficiency and risk reduction, and (2) Head of Procurement who cares about cost and compliance. No webinars or paid social for now -- we don't have the budget or the ops to run those consistently.
evals.expected_output: An 8-week content calendar in table format (Week, Date, Channel, Content Title, Format, Persona, CTA, Status) with per-week summaries including key theme, pillar link, and production deadline. Should show a clear pillar-cluster structure with at least 2 pillar assets across the 8 weeks (one pre-launch, one post-launch) and 4-8 cluster pieces derived from each. All content mapped to either the VP Legal Ops or Head of Procurement persona. Production deadlines set 5+ business days before publish dates. Weekly output within the capacity of 1 full-time writer with AI assistance (roughly 6-9 pieces/week maximum). LegalTech Connect in week 3 used as a content amplification moment. No webinar or paid social content planned since those channels are uncommitted.
expectations: Pipeline generation: every content piece includes a CTA that maps to the campaign goal of demo requests -- pre-launch pieces drive toward waitlist/early access signups that feed the demo pipeline, post-launch pieces drive direct demo booking, and no piece exists without a conversion-oriented CTA tied to one of the two personas (VP Legal Ops or Head of Procurement)

Content leverage: pillar-cluster model is clearly visible with at least 2 cornerstone assets (one pre-launch, one post-launch) each generating 4-8 derived cluster pieces across blog, LinkedIn, and email -- demonstrating maximum content output from minimum production effort by a single writer

Execution velocity: total weekly content volume stays within 6-9 pieces per week (1 full-time writer with AI multiplier), and any week approaching the ceiling is flagged with a recommendation to cut or defer lower-priority pieces -- prioritizing pillar content and demo-driving assets over channel fill

Execution velocity: every production deadline is set at least 5 business days before the corresponding publish date, and LegalTech Connect in week 3 has pre-event teasers, live coverage, and post-event recap content with deadlines that account for the event timeline -- ensuring the tentpole moment is not missed due to production delays

Alignment: content is split between the two personas with messaging tailored to each buyer's priorities -- VP Legal Ops content emphasizes efficiency and risk reduction, Head of Procurement content emphasizes cost and compliance -- so the calendar produces assets the sales team can use in persona-specific outreach

Content leverage: only the four committed channels appear (LinkedIn company page, CEO LinkedIn, blog, email newsletter) with NO webinar or paid social content planned, and channel cadence stays within sustainable defaults (LinkedIn company page up to 3x/week, CEO LinkedIn up to 4-5x/week, blog 1-2x/week, email 1-2x/month) -- preventing the team from overcommitting to channels they cannot sustain

evals.prompt: We need a quarterly content plan (13 weeks) but here's the constraint -- we only have 1 part-time content person, no AI tools approved yet, and she's also handling our event logistics so realistically she can do maybe 1-2 pieces per week max. Company is a cybersecurity consultancy targeting CISOs and IT Directors at financial services firms (banks, insurance, fintech). Goal is thought leadership that drives inbound discovery calls. We only want to commit to LinkedIn company page and a monthly blog post -- we tried email newsletters last year and couldn't keep it up so I'd rather not plan for that. If there's a smarter way to structure this with our constraints I'm open to it but don't plan content we can't actually produce.
evals.expected_output: A 13-week quarterly content calendar constrained to 1-2 pieces per week maximum, covering only LinkedIn company page and blog. Should include 2-3 pillar assets spaced across the quarter with minimal cluster pieces per pillar given capacity limits. Calendar should acknowledge the resource constraint explicitly, possibly recommending content repurposing strategies to maximise impact from limited output. No email newsletter planned. Every piece targeted at CISOs or IT Directors in financial services with CTAs driving discovery calls.
expectations: Pipeline generation: every content piece targets either the CISO or IT Director persona at financial services firms and includes a CTA that drives toward discovery calls (e.g., 'Schedule a security assessment', 'Book a 30-min consultation') -- no piece exists without both a persona assignment and a conversion-oriented CTA that feeds the inbound pipeline

Execution velocity: total content volume never exceeds 1-2 pieces in any single week, and the output includes a workload calculation confirming that 13-26 total pieces over the quarter is within part-time writer capacity -- with specific weeks flagged as lighter or buffer weeks to account for event logistics responsibilities

Content leverage: pillar-cluster model is adapted to the constraint with 2-3 pillar assets spaced every 4-6 weeks across the 13-week quarter, each generating a reduced cluster of 2-4 derived pieces rather than the full 4-8, with an explicit explanation of this tradeoff between ideal cadence and actual capacity -- maximizing discovery-call-driving output from minimum production

Budget efficiency: only LinkedIn company page and blog are planned, with NO email newsletter, webinar, paid social, or CEO LinkedIn content -- and the plan explicitly acknowledges that email was dropped due to prior inability to sustain it, preventing resource waste on a channel the team has already proven they cannot maintain

Alignment: content topics are specifically relevant to cybersecurity concerns at financial services firms (regulatory compliance, threat landscape for banks/insurance/fintech, vendor risk management) rather than generic cybersecurity thought leadership -- so the content positions the consultancy as a specialist that CISOs and IT Directors at these firms would want to talk to

Execution velocity: production deadlines are set at least 5 business days before publish dates, and the calendar includes a content repurposing strategy that explains how each pillar asset gets broken into LinkedIn posts and blog derivatives -- so the part-time writer produces one asset and gets multiple channel outputs from it

evals.prompt: We just published a major whitepaper called 'The State of Supply Chain Visibility 2026' -- 40 pages, original survey data from 500 logistics leaders, covers predictive analytics adoption, real-time tracking maturity, and supplier risk scoring. I want to build a 6-week content calendar that squeezes maximum value out of this one asset. Target personas are (1) VP Supply Chain at enterprise manufacturers and (2) Director of Logistics at 3PL providers. Channels: LinkedIn company page, CEO LinkedIn (our CEO was quoted extensively in the report), blog, email to our subscriber list of ~8,000, and one webinar. We have 1 full-time writer with AI assistance. Campaign goal is whitepaper downloads that feed into our ABM nurture. Key stat from the report: '73% of supply chain leaders say they lack real-time visibility into Tier 2+ suppliers' -- that's our headline hook.
evals.expected_output: A 6-week content calendar built entirely around the 'State of Supply Chain Visibility 2026' whitepaper as the single pillar asset. Should include 8+ cluster content pieces derived from the whitepaper's three themes (predictive analytics, real-time tracking, supplier risk scoring). The '73% lack visibility' stat should appear as a hook across multiple pieces. Content mapped across all five committed channels with cadence within sustainable defaults. Webinar planned around a key finding. All pieces drive whitepaper downloads as the primary CTA.
expectations: Pipeline generation: the primary CTA across nearly all content pieces is whitepaper download that feeds into the ABM nurture, and the calendar explicitly shows how downloads flow into the nurture sequence -- connecting content output to pipeline entry rather than treating downloads as a terminal metric

Content leverage: pillar-cluster model produces at least 8 derived cluster pieces from the single whitepaper, organized around its three sub-themes (predictive analytics adoption, real-time tracking maturity, supplier risk scoring) so each theme generates multiple pieces across different channels and formats -- demonstrating maximum content output from one 40-page research asset

Content leverage: the headline stat ('73% of supply chain leaders lack real-time visibility into Tier 2+ suppliers') is repurposed as a hook across multiple channels and formats -- LinkedIn posts, blog headlines, email subject lines, webinar promotion -- demonstrating how one data point gets repackaged to drive downloads from different audience touchpoints

Alignment: every content piece is mapped to either the VP Supply Chain at enterprise manufacturers or the Director of Logistics at 3PL providers, with messaging tailored to each persona's distinct priorities -- so the content calendar produces persona-specific assets that sales can use in ABM account outreach

Execution velocity: channel cadence matches sustainable defaults for all five committed channels (LinkedIn company page up to 3x/week, CEO LinkedIn up to 4-5x/week leveraging CEO quotes, blog 1-2x/week, email 1-2x across 6 weeks to the 8K list, exactly 1 webinar mid-campaign as a mid-funnel conversion event) and total weekly volume stays within 6-9 pieces for 1 writer with AI assistance

Pipeline generation: the webinar is structured as a mid-funnel conversion event built around a key whitepaper finding, with a registration CTA that funnels attendees to the whitepaper as a follow-up asset and into the ABM nurture -- not a standalone event disconnected from the download-to-pipeline motion

Execution velocity: production deadlines are set at least 5 business days before every publish date, and the 6-week sequence is ordered so high-leverage pieces (pillar derivatives, webinar promotion, email sends to 8K list) are front-loaded while lower-leverage channel fill is scheduled later -- ensuring the highest-impact content ships first
